skill.name: global-validation
skill.description: Implement server-side validation with allowlists, specific error messages, type checking, and sanitization to prevent security vulnerabilities and ensure data integrity. Use this skill when creating or editing form request classes, when validating API inputs, when implementing validation rules in controllers or services, when writing client-side validation for user experience, when sanitizing user input to prevent injection attacks, when validating business rules, when implementing error message display, or when ensuring consistent validation across all application entry points.
skill.summary: Guide AI coding agents to implement consistent validation across forms, APIs, services, and background jobs.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, path traversal, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are documentation false positives. The skill contains validation guidance and a relative Markdown link, with no executable code, network access, shell execution, or prompt injection text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Path Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static command finding points to a Laravel directory path inside Markdown backticks. It is documentation text, not Ruby backtick execution or a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a backticked directory path in a bullet list. No executable command or interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings point to a relative Markdown link for reference material. There is no file read, write, inclusion, or runtime path handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../../../ sequence appears only in a Markdown link. The skill does not instruct the agent to access arbitrary user-controlled paths.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings on the description and overview lines have no supporting evidence. The text discusses validation and data integrity, not hash algorithms or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No weak algorithm name, cryptographic operation, or secret handling appears on the cited lines. The signal appears to be triggered by generic security wording.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Business Rule Example Treated as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to an example about validating business rules. It does not request host, user, network, process, or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a validation example using balances and date ranges. It has no semantic connection to system enumeration.

content.value_statement: Applications often collect input through many paths, which can create inconsistent rules and unclear errors. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to apply server-side validation, allowlists, type checks, sanitization, and useful messages.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of form request classes for validation logic.
Promotes allowlist-based rules for forms, APIs, and services.
Directs agents to validate types, formats, ranges, and required fields.
Encourages sanitization for SQL injection, XSS, and command injection risks.
Supports specific, actionable validation error messages.
Covers validation for uploads, custom rules, and business rules.
limitations: Provides guidance only and does not include executable validation code.
Depends on the project framework and existing validation patterns.
Does not replace manual review of security-sensitive validation rules.
References external project standards that may not exist in every checkout.

use_cases.title: Add Form Request Validation
use_cases.description: Use the skill to guide creation or updates of request validation for web forms.

use_cases.title: Harden API Inputs
use_cases.description: Use the skill to validate API payloads with required fields, formats, ranges, and business rules.

use_cases.title: Review Validation Coverage
use_cases.description: Use the skill to check whether forms, APIs, jobs, and services apply consistent validation.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Form
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single user-facing form.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to add server-side validation for this form. Include required fields, type checks, and clear error messages.

prompt_templates.title: Review API Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for an API endpoint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to review this API endpoint. Identify missing validation rules, unsafe input handling, and unclear error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Design Business Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to design validation for these business rules. Cover allowed values, boundary cases, authorization-sensitive checks, and user-facing errors.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Entry Points
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request across a feature or service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to audit all input entry points for this feature. Compare forms, APIs, services, uploads, and jobs for consistent validation.

output: A request validation plan with required name and email fields.
Type and format checks for each input.
Specific messages for missing, invalid, or disallowed values.

output_examples.input: Review an API endpoint that accepts file uploads.
output: Server-side rules that do not rely on client-side validation.
Error messages that explain how to fix each rejected upload.
Business rule checks for valid dates and sufficient balance.
Boundary cases for past dates, zero values, and invalid ranges.
Consistent validation guidance for web, API, and job entry points.

best_practices: Use server-side validation as the source of truth for every input path.
Prefer allowlists and explicit type checks over broad rejection rules.
Write error messages that explain the field, problem, and expected correction.
anti_patterns: Relying only on client-side validation for security-sensitive inputs.
Using vague validation errors that hide the field or expected format.
Applying different rules to forms, APIs, and background jobs for the same data.

faq.question: Does this skill generate validation code?
faq.answer: It guides the AI agent while it writes or reviews validation code in your project.

faq.question: Can it help with Laravel form requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance specifically covers form request classes and validation logic.

faq.question: Does it replace security review?
faq.answer: No. Security-sensitive rules still need human review and testing.

faq.question: Can it handle API validation?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers API inputs, required fields, formats, ranges, and error responses.

faq.question: Does it support client-side validation?
faq.answer: It can guide client-side validation for user experience, while keeping server-side validation authoritative.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
